steps.title: Pick easy values for the ratio
steps.content: Since $b$ is $87\%$ of $c$, choose $c=100$ and $b=87$. This keeps the ratio exact and avoids decimal algebra.

steps.title: Find a as a multiple of b
Calculate a/b and convert to percentage
steps.content:
Divide by $b=87$:
$$\frac{a}{b}=\frac{24\cdot87\cdot187}{100\cdot87}=\frac{24\cdot187}{100}=44.88$$
So $a$ is $44.88\cdot100\%=4{,}488\%$ of $b$. That matches D.
